user_query:
I’m consolidating my personal relationship info into a small, file-based directory. Please read all of these inputs under input/:
- input/contacts.json
- input/relationships.csv
- input/notes.jsonl
- input/stale_thresholds.yaml
- input/as_of.txt

Using those inputs, build the following artifacts:
1) Generate Obsidian-friendly Markdown people cards under output/people/, one per person, named with a leading underscore and a slugified name (lowercased, hyphenated). Each card must follow this structure:
   - First line: "# <Display Name>"
   - Bullet lines for: Tags (as space-separated #tags, all lowercased), Org, Role, Met, Last contact, Tier
   - A "## Notes" section with one line per note formatted "- YYYY-MM-DD — <text>" (use the notes provided; if a note’s date is later than the person’s current last_contact, update the person’s Last contact to the note’s date)
   - A "## Connections" section listing one line per connection the person is part of, formatted "- [[Other Name]] — <label>"
2) Generate a machine-readable graph index at output/people/_graph.json with two top-level keys: "nodes" (keyed by slug) and "edges". Each node must include: displayName, tags (array), org, role, met, lastContact, tier, notes (array of objects with date and text), file (the filename with leading underscore). Each edge must include: from (slug), to (slug), label.
3) Export a Mermaid diagram to output/people/mermaid.md using the syntax printed by a typical "graph LR" export: one line per node defining the slug and displayName, and one line per edge, including the label in quotes when present.
4) Compute a staleness report as of the date in input/as_of.txt using the thresholds in input/stale_thresholds.yaml. Use the person’s effective last contact (after applying any note-based updates). Consider a person stale only if days_since_last_contact > threshold for their tier. Write a chat-friendly digest to output/stale_report.txt in this exact message-style format:
   - First line: "🔔 *Relationship check-in*"
   - Then one line per stale person, sorted by tier in the order: close, regular, acquaintance. Use these emojis by tier: close=❤️, regular=👋, acquaintance=📇. Each line must be formatted: "<emoji> *<Name>* (<tier>) — last contact: <Nd ago>"
   - End with: "Consider reaching out to keep these connections warm!"

Details:
- Normalize names to slugs using lowercase hyphenation (e.g., "Alex Chen" → "alex-chen"); use the original display name in Markdown and graph index.
- Tags must be lowercased in both Markdown and JSON.
- Ensure bidirectional connections are reflected correctly in Markdown via the Connections section on both involved people.
- Do not write any other files besides the ones requested under output/.
